## Summary

Natively 2.0.9 — Natively API integration, audio transcription root-cause fix, native module stability, and integrated community PRs.

## What's New

- Natively API tier with hosted STT (Deepgram/Google) and AI routing — activate with an API key
- Inline usage dashboard showing transcription, AI, and search quota with progress bars and reset date
- Dual-channel Natively API STT — system audio and microphone connect simultaneously using per-channel session keys, eliminating concurrent_session_blocked errors
- Dodo Payments integration with HMAC-SHA256 webhook verification, idempotent replay guard, and subscription lifecycle handling

## Improvements

- LLM resilience — 60s/3-retry for OpenAI and 90s/3-retry for Claude; Natively API falls back to Gemini on failure
- GoogleSTT proactive restart at 4m 30s to avoid the 5-minute hard gRPC limit
- REST STT audio resampled to 16kHz mono before upload — ~6x smaller files, under Groq/OpenAI 25MB limit
- Deepgram reconnect capped at 10 attempts to prevent infinite retry loops
- Windows overlay z-order re-asserted after showInactive() to prevent DWM silently demoting the overlay
- macOS compositor hide delay increased from 50ms to 150ms to prevent black frames on screenshot capture
- STT provider key inputs now correctly pre-populate with saved values across all providers
- Custom AI provider requests now have a 30s AbortController timeout to prevent indefinite hangs

## Fixes

- Fixed the audio transcription issue — Deepgram was receiving raw PCM without encoding=linear16, causing immediate upstream_closed and zero transcription
- Fixed STT over-billing — mic-channel was double-billed every session; short reconnect sessions now have a 30s threshold before billing; Math.ceil replaced with Math.round
- Fixed native module crash — better-sqlite3 rebuilt for Electron NMV 130, resolving NODE_MODULE_VERSION 141 vs 130 crash affecting all DB operations
- Fixed asar stub audio silence — app.asar.unpacked path now checked first; added getInputDevices() smoke-test to catch false-pass
- Fixed ad-hoc signing order — codesign --deep runs before .node binary re-signing to preserve screen-capture entitlement
- Fixed keybind registration failure — broadcasts keybinds:registration-failed IPC event to the renderer when a shortcut cannot be registered
- Fixed dark mode logo — force-black-icon CSS class now renders correctly; resolved opacity specificity conflict
- Fixed theme flash on launch — data-theme applied synchronously from localStorage before JS bundle loads
- Fixed Natively STT fallback — stored sttProvider='natively' with no key now gracefully falls back to GoogleSTT instead of crashing
- Integrated several PRs — napi-rs v3 migration, Windows header layout fix, platform-aware shortcut symbols, TypeScript type fixes across electron.d.ts, preload.ts, and ipcHandlers.ts

## Technical

- Migrated native audio entrypoints to napi-rs v3 ThreadsafeFunction API with err-first (err, arg) callback signatures
- nativelyapi/server.js comprehensive rewrite fixing 15 critical bugs including missing imports, ReferenceError constants, session key scoping, and HMAC webhook verification
- scripts/build-native.js — dynamic Xcode clang version resolution fixes x86_64 cross-compilation linker error on Xcode 16+
- Added NSScreenCaptureUsageDescription and NSMicrophoneUsageDescription to mac.extendInfo for correct TCC permission dialogs
- nativelyapi/railway.toml deployment config and test-prod.sh production smoke-test added

## ⚠️macOS Installation (Unsigned Build)

Download the correct architecture .zip or .dmg file for your device (Apple Silicon or Intel).

If you see "App is damaged":

- **For .zip downloads:**
  1. Move the app to your Applications folder.
  2. Open Terminal and run: `xattr -cr /Applications/Natively.app`

- **For .dmg downloads:**
  1. Open Terminal and run:
     ```bash
     xattr -cr ~/Downloads/Natively-2.0.9-arm64.dmg
     # Or for Intel Macs:
     xattr -cr ~/Downloads/Natively-2.0.9-x64.dmg
     ```
  2. Install the natively.dmg
  3. Open Terminal and run: `xattr -cr /Applications/Natively.app`

## ⚠️Windows Installation (Unsigned Build)

When running the installer on Windows, you might see a "Windows protected your PC" warning from Microsoft Defender SmartScreen saying it prevented an unrecognized app from starting. 

Since this is an unsigned build, this is expected. You can safely ignore it by clicking **More info** and then **Run anyway**.

\\ refer to change.md for detailed changes
